metadata.description: Enterprise Knowledge Graph — Foundation Synthesis across BA, DA, TA, AA layers
metadata.confidence_schema: HIGH = direct code evidence confirmed by Agent 2; MEDIUM = inferred from structure; LOW = assumed from convention

rules.rule: Order records snapshot product name, picture, and catalogue ID at purchase time. Future catalogue changes do not alter order history.
On login, anonymous basket items merge into account basket; anonymous basket permanently deleted.
Placing an order requires a non-empty basket. Basket permanently deleted after order saved.
Basket add without explicit quantity defaults to quantity 1.
Only ADMINISTRATORS role may create, update, or delete catalogue products.
Anonymous shoppers may add to basket; only authenticated shoppers may proceed to checkout.
Admin API authentication uses JWT tokens carrying user name and all assigned roles as claims.
Email notification system is non-functional — all send calls silently return without delivering any message.
Every catalogue browse API request includes a mandatory 1-second artificial delay. This is a known performance defect in production code.
Admin panel caches product list in browser localStorage for 1 minute; any write immediately clears and reloads.
Order.BuyerId matches Buyer.IdentityGuid by string value convention — no database foreign key.
Orders have no status field. Once created, orders are immutable and cannot be updated, cancelled, or progressed.
rules.evidence: BA:04_business_rules.md BR-12; Order.cs — no status property

rules.rule: Demo shopper and administrator accounts seeded on startup with hardcoded default password.
Domain entity constructors use guard clauses to enforce invariants at object creation, before data reaches database.
All orders record hardcoded shipping address: 123 Main St., Kent, OH, United States, 44240.
Anonymous shoppers identified by 10-year GUID cookie marked as essential and not subject to consent banners.
Basket transfer at login only occurs if cookie value is a valid GUID.
Only authenticated shoppers may access checkout — unauthenticated visitors redirected to login.
Basket must contain at least one item to proceed to checkout.
Catalogue product names must be unique.
Catalogue product price must be greater than zero.
Product name and description must not be empty at creation and update.
New catalogue products always receive a default placeholder image; direct image upload from admin UI is permanently disabled.
JWT tokens expire 7 days after issue.
Account lockout enabled — repeated failed password attempts lock the account.
Checkout form submission updates basket item quantities before order is created.
New user registration does not require email confirmation — account activated immediately after registration.
Registration requires valid email, password 6-100 characters, and matching confirmation password.
Default seeded account passwords are hardcoded plaintext constants in source code — must not use in production.
rules.evidence: BA:04_business_rules.md BR-29; AuthorizationConstants.cs:8 — explicit TODO comment

rules.rule: Shoppers can only view their own order history and order detail — other accounts return not-found.
Initial catalogue seeded with 5 brands, 4 types, 12 products — skipped if data already exists.
JWT signing secret key hardcoded as plaintext constant in source code — must not use in production.
rules.evidence: BA:04_business_rules.md BR-32; AuthorizationConstants.cs:12 — explicit TODO comment

rules.rule: Shipping address fields have database-enforced max lengths: postcode 18, street 180, state 60, country 90, city 100 characters.
rules.evidence: BA:04_business_rules.md BR-33; DA:erd.md confirmed from OrderConfiguration.cs

rules.rule: Payment method must never store full card details — only PCI-compliant token, alias, and last 4 digits.
rules.evidence: BA:04_business_rules.md BR-34; PaymentMethod.cs:7 — explicit PCI comment referencing Stripe

rules.rule: Buyer aggregate and PaymentMethod entity are structurally defined but entirely dormant — no active service layer creates or queries them.
rules.evidence: BA:04_business_rules.md BR-35; DA:erd.md CONFIRMED DEAD — not registered in CatalogContext

rules.rule: Catalogue seeding retries up to 10 times on database failure before aborting application startup.
Identity seeding does not check if ADMINISTRATORS role exists before creating it — produces duplicate role error on restart.

value_streams.name: Catalogue Lifecycle (Admin)
critical_gaps: 1-second artificial delay on every catalogue load BR-09

value_streams.name: New User Onboarding
critical_gaps: Email confirmation silently discarded BR-08, BR-27

wave_1_critical_blockers.action: Implement transactional email delivery (SendGrid or SMTP)
Externalise secrets: move JWT key and default passwords to environment variables or Azure Key Vault
Remove 1-second artificial catalogue delay: delete await Task.Delay(1000) from CatalogItemListPagedEndpoint.cs:42
Integrate payment processing: activate Buyer aggregate with Stripe or Braintree

wave_2_high_priority.action: Add order status lifecycle: PLACED, PROCESSING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED
Add inventory management: StockQuantity field on CatalogItem with checkout validation

wave_3_medium.action: Fix identity seeding idempotency: wrap role creation in existence check
Cache brand and type lookups separately in BlazorAdmin

entities.evidence: DA:erd.md; DA:review-summary CORRECTED-1 — nvarchar(100) not nvarchar(max)

entities.name: Basket

key_fields.BuyerId: nvarchar(256) — GUID for anonymous, username for authenticated

pii: PII-07: BuyerId is identity reference; sensitivity may escalate to HIGH if BuyerId = email (OQ-001)

key_fields.CatalogItemId: int (soft ref — no DB FK confirmed)
key_fields.UnitPrice: decimal (price locked at basket-add time — does not update)

entities.name: Order
pii: PII-05: BuyerId HIGH if email; MEDIUM if GUID
PII-06: ShipToAddress_* HIGH sensitivity (full physical address)
entities.note: All current orders have hardcoded address 123 Main St Kent OH per BR-15

entities.name: Buyer
entities.evidence: DA:erd.md CONFIRMED DEAD — not registered in CatalogContext; BA:05_data_model.md; BR-35

key_fields.IdentityGuid: string non-nullable (matches Order.BuyerId by value convention — no DB FK)

entities.name: PaymentMethod
entities.evidence: DA:erd.md CONFIRMED DEAD; BR-34; PaymentMethod.cs:7 explicit PCI comment referencing Stripe

key_fields.CardId: string? nullable (PCI-compliant token ONLY — no raw card data)

entities.evidence: DA:erd.md value object; BA:05_data_model.md owned entity

entities.evidence: DA:erd.md value object; BA:05_data_model.md — snapshot pattern
entities.note: Value object capturing product state at purchase — immutable; immune to future catalogue changes

entities.evidence: BA:05_data_model.md — parent of all domain entities

repositories.name: CatalogDatabase (Microsoft.eShopOnWeb.CatalogDb)
repositories.technology: SQL Server — Azure SQL Edge (Docker/EOL) or LocalDB (dev)
repositories.id_strategy: HiLo sequences for CatalogItem/Brand/Type; IDENTITY for Basket/Order

repositories.name: IdentityDatabase (Microsoft.eShopOnWeb.Identity)
repositories.technology: SQL Server — Azure SQL Edge (Docker/EOL) or LocalDB (dev)

cross_db_soft_references.risk: Orphan baskets accumulate if user deleted; GDPR erasure gap
No FK cascade; order history preserved after user deletion (may be intentional for financial records)
Orphan basket items if catalogue item deleted
Mitigated — ProductName and PictureUri captured at order time; display survives catalogue item deletion

caching.name: BlazorAdmin localStorage Cache (Admin Catalog)
caching.invalidation: Write-through for items (RefreshLocalStorageList on Create/Edit/Delete); TTL-only for brands/types

pii_summary.sensitivity: MEDIUM (escalates to HIGH if BuyerId = email — OQ-001)
LOW (escalates to MEDIUM/HIGH if BuyerId = email — OQ-001)

architecture_violations.description: Module dependency cycle: Admin -> ApplicationCore -> Basket -> Catalog -> DataAccess -> Identity -> Order -> Web
EfRepository has high coupling score of 16
UriComposer has high coupling score of 8
ApplicationCore references BlazorShared — domain layer depends on UI-shared library, violating Clean Architecture dependency rule

nfr_gaps: No retry or circuit breaker on BlazorAdmin HTTP calls to PublicApi (TD-07)
No EF Core retry strategy for transient SQL errors (TD-09)
No health check endpoints confirmed in either service (TD-08)
Docker Compose depends_on has no health-gate — startup race condition (TD-11)
Identity password minimum length of 6 is below NIST SP 800-63B minimum of 8 (TD-21)
No distributed tracing or observability library declared

assumptions.assumption: BuyerId in Basket and Order stores the user's email address, not a GUID. Unit test TransferBasket.cs uses 'testuser@microsoft.com' as BuyerId.
assumptions.impact: If confirmed, PII-05 and PII-07 should be elevated to HIGH sensitivity

assumptions.assumption: No GDPR right-to-erasure workflow exists in the codebase — none was found in any extracted file.
assumptions.impact: Significant compliance gap if confirmed absent

assumptions.assumption: Azure SQL Edge EOL container is used only for local development — production uses Azure SQL Database.
assumptions.impact: If Azure SQL Edge is in production, security patch coverage gap

assumptions.assumption: A CORS policy for BlazorAdmin-to-PublicApi cross-origin calls exists in Program.cs startup code not captured in config file analysis.
assumptions.impact: If absent, browser blocks all cross-origin admin API calls or AllowAnyOrigin is in use

assumptions.assumption: Swagger UI is gated behind IsDevelopment() check in Program.cs — not served in production.
assumptions.impact: If not gated, full API surface map is publicly accessible without authentication

assumptions.assumption: No scheduled jobs, background services, or message queue consumers exist — none were detected in static analysis.
assumptions.impact: If a basket expiry job exists but was not captured, abandoned basket accumulation risk (G1-06) may already be addressed

assumptions.assumption: The system is the well-known eShopOnWeb Microsoft reference implementation. The AA pipeline reported system_name as 'unknown' because no authoritative name artifact was found in parsed source code.
assumptions.evidence: BA layer headers; TA layer headers; AA:application-architecture-summary.md

normalization_log.description: CatalogItem appears in BA (business concept 'Product'), DA (technical entity CatalogItem), TA (component), AA (entity candidate). Merged as DATA-ENT-001 with DA as owner_layer.
normalization_log.resolution: DATA-ENT-001 is canonical. Business concept name 'Product' recorded in business_concept field.

normalization_log.description: DA Agent 1 reported all string columns as nvarchar(max). DA Agent 2 corrected 11 columns by reading EF IEntityTypeConfiguration files (CatalogBrandConfiguration.cs, CatalogTypeConfiguration.cs, etc.).
normalization_log.resolution: All column lengths use DA Agent 2 confirmed values. This is a HIGH impact correction affecting migration script generation.

normalization_log.description: BA listed 'Buyer Account Structure' as Core active domain. DA Agent 2 confirmed DEAD (Buyer and PaymentMethod have no DbSet registration in CatalogContext).
normalization_log.resolution: DATA-ENT-008, DATA-ENT-009, DATA-AGG-003 all marked DORMANT with HIGH confidence. BA capability BIZ-CAP-030 and BIZ-CAP-031 likewise marked DORMANT.

normalization_log.description: DA Agent 1 described BlazorAdmin cache as 'custom in-process cache'. DA Agent 2 corrected to browser localStorage (Blazored.LocalStorage) with confirmed 1-minute TTL and write-through invalidation.
normalization_log.resolution: CACHE-002 uses confirmed technology. TTL and invalidation strategy are accurate. This changes scope and isolation properties significantly.

normalization_log.description: DA Agent 1 listed 'Buyers' as a named role. DA Agent 2 found no such role constant anywhere. Only 'Administrators' role exists (confirmed from BlazorShared/Authorization/Constants.cs).
normalization_log.resolution: BIZ-ACT-003 uses confirmed role string 'Administrators'. No Buyers role node was created.

normalization_log.description: DA Agent 1 stated Web MVC reads CatalogItem directly from database. DA Agent 2 found IMemoryCache layer (CachedCatalogViewModelService, 30s sliding TTL) wrapping all catalog reads.
normalization_log.resolution: CACHE-001 added. APP-SVC-014 (CachedCatalogViewModelService) added. This is architecturally significant — there are TWO independent cache layers (server-side IMemoryCache for Web MVC; client-side localStorage for BlazorAdmin).

normalization_log.description: DA Agent 1 implied DuplicateException thrown when same item added to basket twice. DA Agent 2 confirmed silent auto-merge: Basket.AddItem() increments quantity on existing item.
normalization_log.resolution: BIZ-CAP-010 note reflects confirmed auto-merge behaviour.

normalization_log.description: Health check endpoints (GET /home_page_health_check, GET /api_health_check) appear in AA evidence (INT-012, INT-013) but TA notes these are unconfirmed as full health check infrastructure (TD-08).
normalization_log.resolution: These are recorded in AA evidence but not created as APP-API nodes — they are minimal route stubs, not production health check implementations. Recorded in open questions as infrastructure gap.

normalization_log.description: AA pattern detection flagged primary style as 'Layered Monolith' (confidence 0.78). BA/TA analysis confirms Clean Architecture (AP-01 — HIGH confidence) with ApplicationCore having zero outbound project references.
normalization_log.resolution: The system is Clean Architecture in intent and structure. AA's 'Layered Monolith' classification reflects the fact that all projects share one deployable and boundary violations exist. Both are accurate at different abstraction levels.

normalization_log.description: CatalogItem.Id key generation appears as 'auto-increment identity' in DA Agent 1. DA Agent 2 corrected to HiLo sequences (catalog_hilo, catalog_brand_hilo, catalog_type_hilo).
normalization_log.resolution: DATA-ENT-001, DATA-ENT-002, DATA-ENT-003 key_fields use confirmed HiLo sequence notation. This affects migration rollback strategies and bulk insert behaviour.

normalization_log.description: TA identified ApplicationCore -> BlazorShared project reference (TD-13). This was not present in BA or DA layers but is architecturally significant as it means the domain layer depends on a UI-shared library.
normalization_log.resolution: ARCH-VIOL-011 added to application.architecture_violations. APP-IF-006 BlazorShared notes this violation.

normalization_log.description: AA pipeline reported system_name as 'unknown'. BA and TA layer headers consistently identify the system as eShopOnWeb.
normalization_log.resolution: All foundation documents use 'eShopOnWeb' as the system name. ASMP-007 records the basis for this choice.

open_questions.question: Does BuyerId in Basket and Order store the user's email address or their GUID (AspNetUsers.Id)?
open_questions.impact: Affects PII sensitivity of PII-05 and PII-07; affects GDPR erasure complexity; unit test evidence suggests email

open_questions.question: Is there a GDPR right-to-erasure / user deletion workflow implemented anywhere in the codebase (background job, admin action, scheduled task)?
open_questions.impact: Significant GDPR compliance gap if absent

open_questions.question: What is the data retention policy for Orders and OrderItems (financial records)? Is there a purge or archive schedule?
open_questions.impact: Legal/compliance requirement; determines whether a purge job is needed

open_questions.question: Does SQL Server TDE (Transparent Data Encryption) apply to either CatalogDatabase or IdentityDatabase in any deployment environment?
open_questions.impact: Determines encrypted-at-rest status for all PII fields

open_questions.question: Are the seeded demo credentials (Pass@word1 and JWT secret key 'SecretKeyOfDoomThatMustBeAMinimumNumberOfBytes') rotated before any non-local deployment?
open_questions.impact: Security blocker — anyone with repo access can sign valid JWT tokens and log in as admin

open_questions.question: Is there a basket expiry or purge mechanism (IHostedService, SQL Agent job, Azure Function) that was not found in the extracted source tree?
open_questions.impact: If absent, abandoned baskets accumulate indefinitely — GDPR and storage concern

open_questions.question: Is Redis planned as a distributed cache? The infra/abbreviations.json defines a Redis naming prefix (cacheRedis: redis-) but no Redis client was wired into the application.
open_questions.impact: Determines whether the caching strategy should be redesigned for multi-instance deployments

open_questions.question: What PCI-compliant payment processor (Stripe vs Braintree vs other) is planned for production activation of the dormant BuyerAggregate?
open_questions.impact: Architecture decision required before implementing AO-05; determines PaymentMethod.CardId token format and checkout flow
